question: Is the offence related to counterfeiting?

yes.question: Is the offence related to currency-notes or bank-notes?

yes.name: Counterfeiting coin, Government stamps, currency-notes or bank-notes

no.name: Making or possessing instruments or materials for forging or counterfeiting coin, Government stamp, currency-notes or bank-notes

no.question: Is the offence related to using or possessing forged or counterfeit coin, Government stamp, currency-notes or bank-notes?

yes.name: Using as genuine, forged or counterfeit coin, Government stamp, currency-notes or bank-notes

no.question: Is the offence related to making or using documents resembling currency-notes or bank-notes?

yes.name: Making or using documents resembling currency-notes or bank-notes

no.question: Is the offence related to Government stamps?

yes.question: Is the offence related to removing or erasing Government stamps?

yes.name: Effacing writing from substance bearing Government stamp, or removing from document a stamp used for it, with intent to cause loss to Government

no.name: Using Government stamp known to have been before used

no.question: Is the offence related to erasing marks on Government stamps?

yes.name: Erasure of mark denoting that stamp has been used

group: OF OFFENCES RELATING TO COIN, CURRENCY-NOTES, BANK-NOTES, AND GOVERNMENT STAMPS

_meta.chapter_subtype: OF OFFENCES RELATING TO COIN, CURRENCY-NOTES, BANK-NOTES, AND GOVERNMENT STAMPS

sections.name: Counterfeiting coin, Government stamps, currency-notes or bank-notes.
Using as genuine, forged or counterfeit coin, Government stamp, currency-notes or bank-notes.
Possession of forged or counterfeit coin, Government stamp, currency-notes or bank-notes.
Making or possessing instruments or materials for forging or counterfeiting coin, Government stamp, currency-notes or bank-notes.
Making or using documents resembling currency-notes or bank-notes.
Effacing writing from substance bearing Government stamp, or removing from document a stamp used for it, with intent to cause loss to Government.
Using Government stamp known to have been before used.
Erasure of mark denoting that stamp has been used.
Person employed in mint causing coin to be of different weight or composition from that fixed by law.
Unlawfully taking coining instrument from mint.
